stat_of_week: 0.17 - Shota Imanaga's WHIP of 0.17 across 6.0 IP for the Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs is the most dominant single-pitcher efficiency line of the week, essentially untouchable over his outing
thriller_note: A dead-even tie in which General Hospital dominated pitching volume (70 K, 5 QS) while Buttville held firm offensively behind Elly De La Cruz's monster week, leaving both teams with a half-win and unfinished business heading into a Week 4 rematch on different schedules.
key_storyline: The Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs continue to be the most star-crossed team in the league — Imanaga's near-historic WHIP, solid team pitching, and six stolen bases weren't enough to do more than eke out a 5-4 win, and with the worst luck delta in the league at -0.60 cumulative, the universe seems determined to deny them. They've now gone 0 category wins in Week 2 and keep posting respectable numbers with nothing to show for it in the standings, raising real questions about whether their roster construction is fundamentally misaligned with their opponents' strengths.
lucky_reason: The Benchwarmers posted only 34 strikeouts and a brutal 5.53 ERA from their pitching staff — the weakest pitching performance among all losers and winners alike — yet still managed to collect enough category wins to log a partial win, riding the league's highest luck delta of +0.91 for the week.
unlucky_reason: The Wonderdogs delivered elite pitching — 62 K, a 4.30 ERA, a 1.13 WHIP, 5 QS, and Imanaga's jaw-dropping 0.17 WHIP — and still only won 5 categories against the Bombers, reflecting a -0.60 luck delta that is the worst in the entire league this week.
records_broken: North Correa tied their own season record with 11 stolen bases as a team in Week 3, matching the prior mark also set by North Correa in Week 3 (listed as 11.0, week 3) — no new record was broken this week.
power_rankings: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Led the league with 8 category wins for the second time in three weeks, Max Muncy's 3 HR and Michael Wacha's 0.60 ERA anchored a 31-run offensive week and a 2.11 team ERA — this roster is the class of the league | same

2. Hiroshima Radiation | Exploded for an 8-2 demolition behind Taj Bradley's 17 K, Tarik Skubal's 14 K, and a 75-strikeout team total that matched the league's best-ever single-week mark; Bryce Harper's .640 OBP headline an offense that is suddenly clicking at full volume | up

3. Athletic Park Dragons | James Wood's .655 OBP and 3 HR, Willson Contreras' 9 RBI, and Dansby Swanson's 8 runs all in the same week is as dominant an offensive performance as the league has seen; the pitching needs repair but this offense alone keeps them in the top three | same

4. BaseballPro77 | Jordan Walker's 5 HR week and Logan Gilbert's 2.08 ERA anchored a 5-3 win; this team keeps finding production from unexpected sources and has now won in back-to-back weeks with a deep, versatile roster | same

5. General Hospital | Cam Schlittler's 15 K is a tantalizing glimpse at rotation upside and Kumar Rocker is now in the fold, but a 4-4 tie against the Skidmarks when you threw 70 K and posted 5 NSVH is a painful missed opportunity; the pitching is elite, the offense needs consistency | down

6. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Lost 6-3 but their pitching was genuinely excellent — Kris Bubic's 0.43 WHIP, Brandon Woodruff's return, and a 1.65 ERA with 10 NSVH are signs of a staff quietly becoming one of the league's best; offense is the limiting factor | up

7. Albuquerque Bombers | Brice Turang's .474 OBP and 4 steals combined contributions, Sandy Alcantara's 10 K, and four waiver additions show an active front office; lost 5-4 in a close one but the roster is being aggressively upgraded week over week | same

8. North Correa | Shohei Ohtani's 3 HR and Oneil Cruz's 6 SB are as exciting as any individual performances in the league, and 11 stolen bases as a team ties the season record — but the pitching allowed a 4.73 ERA and just 2 QS, which cost them the matchup | down

9. Riverside Roughnecks | Won 6-3 over a Benchwarmers squad without logging a single quality start — zero QS and a 5.04 ERA should not produce wins, but Trevor Story's 9 RBI and Jackson Merrill's 3 steals carried the offense enough to survive; this team is more fragile than their record suggests | same

10. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Lost 8-2 in a rout and the -0.35 luck delta can't explain away a shutout in most pitching categories; Yordan Alvarez and Gunnar Henderson — wait, Henderson is on the Wombats — the offense has talent but the 0 NSVH week is a structural problem | down

11. Buttville Skidmarks | Elly De La Cruz was electric with 4 SB and 2 HR but the team still couldn't break 22 RBI; a 4-4 tie is better than a loss but the rotation is thin after MacKenzie Gore and the offense needs more than one reliable power source | same

12. Canberra Wombats | Gunnar Henderson's 4 HR and Jacob deGrom's 1.64 ERA were genuinely impressive and yet the team lost 8-2 — when your best individual performers can't move the team score, the supporting cast is too weak; dropping Roki Sasaki is a puzzling decision | down

13. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Won 5-4 despite pitching brilliantly all week, which should feel better than it does; the -0.60 luck delta is a flashing warning sign and the offense — 25 runs, 6 HR — remains too quiet to trust against better opponents, though the waiver activity adding Dollander shows intent | up

14. Benchwarmers | Dropped three pitchers including Scherzer in the same week their staff posted a 5.53 ERA and 34 strikeouts — the roster is being dismantled rather than rebuilt, and without those arms the ceiling drops even further; the +0.91 luck delta this week is the only thing keeping them off the basement floor | same
waiver_highlights: Albuquerque Bombers | B | Added Keaton Winn, Noelvi Marte, Braxton Garrett, Josh Jung; dropped Sam Antonacci, Jonathan Loáisiga | A four-player haul signals urgency and real roster management — Jung and Marte add positional flexibility and upside at the hot corner while Winn and Garrett address rotation depth, though dropping Loáisiga removes a high-leverage reliever the team may regret losing.

Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | B+ | Added Chase Dollander, Justin Bruihl, Erik Miller, Ildemaro Vargas; dropped Jack Flaherty, Justin Sterner, Taylor Clarke, Matt Strahm | An aggressive four-for-four rotation overhaul that dumps the broken remnants of Flaherty and Strahm (who immediately went to BaseballPro77 earlier) in favor of upside arms like Dollander — bold and necessary given the pitching has been their only asset; Vargas adds roster flexibility.

Benchwarmers | D | Added Casey Schmitt; dropped Josh Naylor, Max Scherzer, Javier Assad | Dropping Scherzer and Assad guts an already thin rotation, and replacing them with a utility bat in Schmitt does nothing to fix a staff that just posted a 5.53 ERA — this looks like managed decline rather than a deliberate rebuild.

Canberra Wombats | D | Added JR Ritchie; dropped Roki Sasaki | Trading a legitimate ace-caliber arm in Sasaki for a speculative JR Ritchie pickup is the most head-scratching move of the week; unless there's an injury involved, this dramatically lowers the ceiling of a rotation that was already struggling.

General Hospital | A- | Added Kumar Rocker; dropped Jack Leiter, Janson Junk | Rocker has top-of-rotation stuff and adds a genuine high-upside arm to an already elite staff — dropping Leiter and Junk is painless, and this move could be the difference-maker in a pennant race if Rocker locks in.

Riverside Roughnecks | C | Added Sam Bachman, Victor Vodnik; dropped Mark Vientos | Adding two bullpen arms makes sense for a team with zero quality starts this week, but losing Vientos removes a legitimate power bat from an offense that already ranks near the bottom in HR production.
